question: What was the density of the Xu et al. 2021 ZF-ZS composite sample prepared by reactive infiltration of K2ZrF6 molten salt?
reference_answer: The ZF-ZS composite sample had a density of 3.55 ± 0.05 g/cm³.
excerpts: Table 1 Density and final open porosity of infiltrated composites. Sample Powder ratio (mass ratio) Open porosity(%) Density(g/ cm3) K2ZrF6 K2TiF6 Si Zr NaCl ZF-S 8 8 1 7.6 2.75 ± 0.05 TF-S 8 8 1 8.0 2.81 ± 0.04 TF-ZS 8 4.8 3.2 1 9.3 2.73 ± 0.03 ZF-ZS 8 4.8 3.2 1 10.2 3.55 ± 0.05

question: In Xu et al. 2021, what was the mass ablation rate of the ZF-ZS C/C-UHTC composite when tested under oxyacetylene flame at 2800 degrees Celsius surface temperature for 60 seconds?
reference_answer: The ZF-ZS composite prepared by Xu and colleagues exhibited a mass ablation rate of 2.6 ± 0.02 mg/s.
evidence_any: ZF-ZS Molten salt + RMI O2:1.96 L/s, C2H2:0.696 L/s, Surface 2800 ℃ 60 2.6±0.02 0.82 ±0.04
excerpts: Table 2 Ablation properties of multiphase UHTC modified C/C composites. Materials Modified method Ablation Test Ablation Time (s) Mass ablation rate (mg/s) Linear ablation rate (μm/ s) Ref. ZF-S Molten salt + RMI O2:1.96 L/s, C2H2:0.696 L/s, Surface 2800 ℃ 60 4.6 ±0.05 1.67 ±0.10 Current study ZF-ZS Molten salt + RMI O2:1.96 L/s, C2H2:0.696 L/s, Surface 2800 ℃ 60 2.6±0.02 0.82 ±0.04 Current study TF-S Molten salt + RMI O2:1.96 L/s, C2H2:0.696 L/s, Surface 2800 ℃ 60 17.3±0.58 60.17±1.34 Current study TF-ZS Molten salt + RMI O2:1.96 L/s, C2H2:0.696 L/s, Surface 2800 ℃ 60 16.0±0.70 38.33±5.58 Current study

question: According to Xu et al. 2021 Table 2, what was the linear ablation rate in micrometers per second for the ZF-ZS composite material at a flame temperature of 2800 degrees Celsius?
reference_answer: The ZF-ZS composite had a linear ablation rate of 0.82 ± 0.04 μm/s under oxyacetylene flame ablation.
evidence_any: ZF-ZS Molten salt + RMI O2:1.96 L/s, C2H2:0.696 L/s, Surface 2800 ℃ 60 2.6±0.02 0.82 ±0.04
excerpts: Table 2 Ablation properties of multiphase UHTC modified C/C composites. Materials Modified method Ablation Test Ablation Time (s) Mass ablation rate (mg/s) Linear ablation rate (μm/ s) Ref. ZF-S Molten salt + RMI O2:1.96 L/s, C2H2:0.696 L/s, Surface 2800 ℃ 60 4.6 ±0.05 1.67 ±0.10 Current study ZF-ZS Molten salt + RMI O2:1.96 L/s, C2H2:0.696 L/s, Surface 2800 ℃ 60 2.6±0.02 0.82 ±0.04 Current study

question: What was the mass ablation rate reported by Xu et al. 2021 for the TF-S C/C-Ti3SiC2-SiC composite material tested at 2800 degrees Celsius surface temperature?
reference_answer: The TF-S composite sample had a mass ablation rate of 17.3 ± 0.58 mg/s.
excerpts: Table 2 Ablation properties of multiphase UHTC modified C/C composites. Materials Modified method Ablation Test Ablation Time (s) Mass ablation rate (mg/s) Linear ablation rate (μm/ s) Ref. TF-S Molten salt + RMI O2:1.96 L/s, C2H2:0.696 L/s, Surface 2800 ℃ 60 17.3±0.58 60.17±1.34 Current study TF-ZS Molten salt + RMI O2:1.96 L/s, C2H2:0.696 L/s, Surface 2800 ℃ 60 16.0±0.70 38.33±5.58 Current study

question: In the Xu et al. 2021 study, what was the open porosity percentage of the ZF-S composite prepared by reactive infiltration of K2ZrF6 molten salt?
reference_answer: The ZF-S sample had an open porosity of 7.6%.
excerpts: Table 1 Density and final open porosity of infiltrated composites. Sample Powder ratio (mass ratio) Open porosity(%) Density(g/ cm3) K2ZrF6 K2TiF6 Si Zr NaCl ZF-S 8 8 1 7.6 2.75 ± 0.05 TF-S 8 8 1 8.0 2.81 ± 0.04 TF-ZS 8 4.8 3.2 1 9.3 2.73 ± 0.03 ZF-ZS 8 4.8 3.2 1 10.2 3.55 ± 0.05

question: According to Xu et al. 2021 Table 2, what was the mass ablation rate of the C/C-ZrC-SiC composite prepared by ICVI plus reactive melt infiltration?
reference_answer: The C/C-ZrC-SiC composite prepared by ICVI and RMI had a mass ablation rate of 0.21 mg/s.
evidence_any: C/C-ZrC-SiC ICVI + RMI O2:0.21 L/s, C2H2:0.155 L/s, Flame 2300 ℃ 90 0.21 1.44
excerpts: Table 2 Ablation properties of multiphase UHTC modified C/C composites. Materials Modified method Ablation Test Ablation Time (s) Mass ablation rate (mg/s) Linear ablation rate (μm/ s) Ref. C/C-ZrC-SiC TCVI + PIP O2:0.42 L/s, C2H2:0.31 L/s, Surface 2400 ℃ 120 1.9 12.00 33 C/C-ZrC-SiC TCVI + PIP O2:0.42 L/s, C2H2:0.31 L/s, Flame 3000 ℃ 120 − 0.33 2.79 34 C/C-ZrC-SiC ICVI + RMI O2:0.21 L/s, C2H2:0.155 L/s, Flame 2300 ℃ 90 0.21 1.44 35

question: In the Popov et al. 2024 study, what was the Vickers hardness of the TiB2-SiC ceramic composite with 3.8 wt% reduced graphene oxide additive (sample TSG03)?
reference_answer: The TSG03 composite containing 3.8 wt% reduced graphene oxide had a Vickers hardness of 26.1 ± 1.2 GPa.
excerpts: Table 2 Carbon content, density, mechanical properties, and thermal conductivity of the sintered samples. Sample ID rGO(wt%) Carbon black (wt%) ρ (g/cm3) ρth*(g/cm3) K1 C (MPa⋅m1/2) HV (GPa) k (W/m•K) Reference TS00 - - 3.82 3.81 6.1±0.3 22.5±1 45±1.2 rGO TSG01 1 - 3.88 3.79 6.0±0.3 22.8±1 45±1.3 TSG02 2 - 3.76 3.76 7.4±0.4 22.1±1.2 58±1.4 TSG03 3.8 - 3.85 3.71 6.6±0.3 26.1±1.2 69±1.5 TSG04 7.4 - 3.76 3.60 6.6±0.3 19.5±1 42±3

question: What was the fracture toughness (K1C) value reported by Popov et al. 2024 for the TiB2-SiC composite with 3.8 wt% reduced graphene oxide (TSG03)?
reference_answer: The TSG03 sample with 3.8 wt% rGO demonstrated a fracture toughness of 6.6 ± 0.3 MPa•m^1/2.
excerpts: Table 2 Carbon content, density, mechanical properties, and thermal conductivity of the sintered samples. Sample ID rGO(wt%) Carbon black (wt%) ρ (g/cm3) ρth*(g/cm3) K1 C (MPa⋅m1/2) HV (GPa) k (W/m•K) rGO TSG03 3.8 - 3.85 3.71 6.6±0.3 26.1±1.2 69±1.5 TSG04 7.4 - 3.76 3.60 6.6±0.3 19.5±1 42±3

question: According to Popov et al. 2024 Table 2, what was the thermal conductivity of the TiB2-SiC composite with 3.8 wt% reduced graphene oxide (sample TSG03)?
reference_answer: The TSG03 composite containing 3.8 wt% reduced graphene oxide exhibited a thermal conductivity of 69 ± 1.5 W/m•K.
excerpts: Table 2 Carbon content, density, mechanical properties, and thermal conductivity of the sintered samples. Sample ID rGO(wt%) Carbon black (wt%) ρ (g/cm3) ρth*(g/cm3) K1 C (MPa⋅m1/2) HV (GPa) k (W/m•K) rGO TSG03 3.8 - 3.85 3.71 6.6±0.3 26.1±1.2 69±1.5 TSG04 7.4 - 3.76 3.60 6.6±0.3 19.5±1 42±3

question: In Popov et al. 2024, what was the Vickers hardness of the TiB2-SiC ceramic with 3.8 wt% carbon black additive (sample TSC03)?
reference_answer: The TSC03 sample with 3.8 wt% carbon black had a Vickers hardness of 15.9 ± 0.8 GPa.
excerpts: Table 2 Carbon content, density, mechanical properties, and thermal conductivity of the sintered samples. Sample ID rGO(wt%) Carbon black (wt%) ρ (g/cm3) ρth*(g/cm3) K1 C (MPa⋅m1/2) HV (GPa) k (W/m•K) Carbon black TSC01 - 1 4.06 3.79 5.8±0.6 20.9±0.9 48±1.5 TSC02 - 2 4.10 3.76 5.5±0.8 17.7±1 45±1.5 TSC03 - 3.8 3.93 3.71 6.2±0.2 15.9±0.8 41±1.8 TSC04 - 7.4 3.77 3.60 5.9±0.3 16.4±0.7 45±3

question: What was the Vickers hardness of the reference TiB2-SiC ceramic sample without any carbon additive (TS00) in the Popov et al. 2024 study?
reference_answer: The reference TS00 TiB2-SiC ceramic without carbon additives had a Vickers hardness of 22.5 ± 1 GPa.
excerpts: Table 2 Carbon content, density, mechanical properties, and thermal conductivity of the sintered samples. Sample ID rGO(wt%) Carbon black (wt%) ρ (g/cm3) ρth*(g/cm3) K1 C (MPa⋅m1/2) HV (GPa) k (W/m•K) Reference TS00 - - 3.82 3.81 6.1±0.3 22.5±1 45±1.2
